nodes.label: Phase 2 — depth is not SCFF's lever
nodes.description: Phase 2 (P2.0-P2.6, June 2026): tests whether SCFF's success can move onto depth given the Scap substrate is cheap on depth but SCFF's strength is width. Verdict: energy-goodness SCFF cannot earn depth via transmission or objective; depth bought cheaply by boosted shallow blocks with GD readouts instead.

nodes.label: the depth wall
nodes.description: SCFF features degrade with depth: per-layer probe declines monotonically toward chance on CIFAR-10-flat (slope -0.018/layer, probe 0.23->0.117). Each layer re-clusters by energy/density and sheds class-relevant directions.
nodes.rationale: Every Phase-2 lever must bend this curve upward; it is the reference curve for P2.1+.

nodes.label: the substrate collision (README §0)
nodes.description: SCFF's algorithmic strength is width, but the Scap-capacitor substrate's cheap physical axis is depth (a deep crossbar). Phase 2's job is to test whether these can be reconciled.

nodes.label: Bridge to Phase 3: the one-word-too-strong caveat
nodes.description: Phase 2's conclusion 'intrinsic to forward-only locality' is flagged as one word too strong — the literature (Phase 3 pass) narrows it to 'intrinsic to the energy objective Sigma h^2', not locality. Every energy-goodness result still stands; Phase 3 tests a different objective family, not a re-run.
nodes.rationale: Directly opens Phase 3's question: is the wall intrinsic to locality or only to the energy objective?
